name: Groundnut Chikki
tagline: Peanut-jaggery brittle — niacin, magnesium, copper, plant protein

hero.alt: Glossy amber peanut brittle slabs cut into rectangles on a wooden board
hero.caption: groundnut chikki — peanut-jaggery brittle

steps.title: Roast and skin the peanuts
steps.detail: Dry-roast peanuts on medium for 6–7 minutes till skins crackle and nuts are toasty. Cool slightly. Rub between palms or in a tea towel to remove skins. Roughly crush half.
steps.tip: Skinned peanuts give a cleaner finish; leave skin on if you don't mind the rustic look.

steps.title: Melt the jaggery
steps.detail: Heat ghee in a heavy pan. Add jaggery and stir on low heat until fully melted and smooth (3–4 min). Test: drop a tiny bit into cold water — it should set hard and crack.
steps.tip: Pull from heat the moment the cold-water test passes; over-cook makes the chikki taste burnt.

steps.title: Combine and set
steps.detail: Stir cardamom and peanuts into the molten jaggery quickly. Pour onto a greased flat surface or parchment. Roll smooth with a greased rolling pin while still hot. Score into rectangles. Cool completely before breaking.
steps.tip: Work fast — molten jaggery sets in 60 seconds at room temp.

healthFacts: Peanuts: 25g protein and 16mg niacin (B3) per 100g — strong vegetarian sources.
Magnesium and copper content per serving easily exceeds 20% of DV.
Resveratrol and p-coumaric acid in peanuts support cardiovascular health.
Jaggery has a slower glycemic curve than refined sugar but portion still matters.
